title: Scaffold the okf-core crate and wire it into the workspace
description:
- Create `crates/okf-core/Cargo.toml`: package `okf-core`, `edition = "2024"`, `version = "0.1.0"`; one dependency `serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }` (the model derives Serialize/Deserialize). No other deps.
- Create `crates/okf-core/src/lib.rs` as an initially-empty crate root (module decls added in tasks 2-3). Add a short crate-level doc comment stating this is the single-source OKF frontmatter contract (model + serializer + parser).
- Add `"crates/okf-core"` to the root `Cargo.toml` `[workspace] members` list (currently only `"crates/bastion"`).
- Add `okf-core = { path = "../okf-core" }` to `crates/bastion/Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]`.
- Verify the workspace resolves: `cargo build` builds both members (okf-core is empty but valid).
acceptance_criteria: cargo build succeeds with two workspace members (bastion + okf-core)
crates/bastion/Cargo.toml declares the okf-core path dependency

title: Move the frontmatter parser into okf-core; repoint consumers via re-export
description:
- Create `crates/okf-core/src/parse.rs` and move the parser out of `crates/bastion/src/validate/frontmatter.rs`: the `Frontmatter` struct, the `ParseResult` enum, `extract_frontmatter`, and `parse_frontmatter`. Make all four `pub` (they were `pub`/`pub(crate)`/private in bastion). Move the parser's unit tests (the `extract_frontmatter` cases) with them into `parse.rs`.
- Register the module in `crates/okf-core/src/lib.rs` (`mod parse; pub use parse::{Frontmatter, ParseResult, extract_frontmatter, parse_frontmatter};`).
- In `crates/bastion/src/validate/frontmatter.rs`: delete the moved parser code and add `pub use okf_core::{Frontmatter, ParseResult, extract_frontmatter, parse_frontmatter};` so existing `crate::validate::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter` call sites (serve/status/handoff.rs, serve/status/repo.rs) keep compiling unchanged. Keep `validate_frontmatter` and its tests exactly as-is — it now matches on the re-exported `okf_core::ParseResult`; behavior must not change.
- In `crates/bastion/src/brain/okf.rs:50`: change `crate::validate::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter(content)` to `okf_core::parse_frontmatter(content)` (the block names this consumer explicitly).
- Confirm the whole workspace compiles and the parser tests now run under okf-core while `validate_frontmatter`'s tests still pass in bastion.
acceptance_criteria: Frontmatter/ParseResult/extract_frontmatter/parse_frontmatter are defined only in okf-core and are pub
validate/frontmatter.rs re-exports them and keeps validate_frontmatter with identical behavior (its tests pass unedited)
serve/status/handoff.rs and serve/status/repo.rs compile without edits

title: Move the OKF model + serializer into okf-core; delete the bastion prototype module
description:
- Create `crates/okf-core/src/frontmatter.rs` and move from `crates/bastion/src/okf/mod.rs`: the `OkfFrontmatter` struct (keep the serde derives + attributes verbatim), `serialize_frontmatter`, and the pure helpers `push_scalar`, `push_list`, `yaml_scalar`, `needs_quote`. Make `OkfFrontmatter` and `serialize_frontmatter` `pub`.
- Move all 18 tests from `okf/mod.rs` into `frontmatter.rs`. The round-trip tests reference the parser — repoint them to okf-core's own `parse_frontmatter`/`validate`-free assertions: use `super::` / `crate::parse::parse_frontmatter` for the parse checks. NOTE: three round-trip tests currently also call bastion's `validate_frontmatter`, which does NOT move to okf-core. Replace those assertions with equivalent parse-level checks (e.g. assert the required scalars parse back with the expected values) so the tests stay self-contained within okf-core; do not add a bastion dev-dependency to okf-core.
- Register the module in `crates/okf-core/src/lib.rs` (`mod frontmatter; pub use frontmatter::{OkfFrontmatter, serialize_frontmatter};`).
- Delete `crates/bastion/src/okf/mod.rs` (and the now-empty `crates/bastion/src/okf/` directory) and remove `mod okf;` from `crates/bastion/src/main.rs:17`.
- Confirm no `crate::okf` references remain in the bastion crate and the workspace builds clean.
acceptance_criteria: OkfFrontmatter and serialize_frontmatter are defined in okf-core (pub) with all serializer tests moved and passing
The moved round-trip tests are self-contained in okf-core (no dependency on bastion's validate_frontmatter)
crates/bastion/src/okf/ is deleted and `mod okf;` removed from main.rs; no crate::okf references remain
The bastion crate contains no OkfFrontmatter/serialize_frontmatter definition

title: Validate
description: Run the Validation Commands listed in tasks.md from the workspace root and confirm all pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo build --release`. Confirm the combined test count is not lower than before the extraction (the moved serializer + parser tests still run, now under okf-core).
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test, and cargo build --release all pass at the workspace root
Combined workspace test count is not lower than before the block
